slides.title: setcookie()
slides.content:
# Setting a Cookie

`setcookie(string $name, string $value = "", int $expires = 0, string $path = "", string $domain = "", bool $secure = false, bool $httponly = false): bool`

This function must be called **before any output** (same rule as `session_start()`).

```php
setcookie('username', 'john_doe', time() + 3600); // expires in 1 hour
```

After setting, the cookie will be available in the next request.

slides.title: Setting a Cookie
slides.content:
```php
<?php
// Set a cookie that expires in 7 days
setcookie('theme', 'dark', time() + (86400 * 7), '/'); // '/' means whole site
?>
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
<?php
echo "Cookie 'theme' is set.";
?>
</body>
</html>
```
slides.codeSnippet:
<?php
setcookie('theme', 'dark', time() + (86400 * 7), '/');
?>
<!DOCTYPE html>
<html>
<body>
<?php
echo "Cookie 'theme' is set.";
?>
</body>
</html>

slides.title: Reading Cookies

quizData.question: When does a cookie set with setcookie() become available in $_COOKIE?

options.text: On the next request from the browser

quizData.explanation: The cookie is sent to the browser and will be returned on subsequent requests.
